story_angle: somber obituary for a lost era or institution
headline: Obituary for the Royal Astronautical Society: A Celestial Dream Extinguished
deck: As a once-great institution fades into history, the echoes of unrealized ambitions linger in our industrious hearts.
article:
In this somber moment, we gather to honor the Royal Astronautical Society, founded in 1861, which once served as a beacon for those daring enough to pursue the celestial threads of ambition. Its dissolution casts a shadow over our aspirations, leaving behind dreams now buried beneath the weight of progress.

During its peak, the Society inspired countless individuals, igniting imaginations that reached beyond earthly confines. Guided by pioneers such as Sir Augustus M. L. Fotheringham and esteemed astronomer Lady Arabella Wright, the Society cultivated a community of thinkers eager to explore the mysteries of interstellar travel. Unfortunately, the relentless advance of a burgeoning industry, intertwined with the very machines meant to elevate humanity among the stars, hastened its decline into obscurity.

As the smoke-filled streets of London buzzed with industrial activity, those who once envisioned a future rich with interplanetary adventures found themselves overshadowed by society's indifference to lofty pursuits. The Society's celebrated endeavors were gradually stifled, leaving dreamers to mourn amidst the haze of industry. The noble ambitions that once fueled our quest for knowledge have become mere whispers in the winds of time.

The Royal Astronautical Society's closure marks the end of an era—one in which visionary thinkers dared to challenge the heavens with sheer will and creativity. In this moment of reflection, we must confront an unsettling reality: in our relentless pursuit of industrial advancement, we may have sacrificed the dreams that once filled our hearts with hope. As we bid farewell to this venerable institution, let us consider the cost of our progress and the aspirations now relegated to the shadows of history.
author: Staff Correspondent

oped.title: Let the Stars Rot: Empire Over Ether
oped.author: Viscountess Eliza Crowe
oped.body: The Royal Astronautical Society did not fade away due to neglect but rather from its own absurdity. In 1890, while coal smoke suffocated our cities and the Raj demanded rifles, these cravat-clad dreamers pursued fantastical visions of 1950. Good riddance. Let their telescopes gather dust. Conquest belongs to gunboats, not rockets that would only elevate our vices. The celestial dream was always a coward’s escape from the realities of London’s slums and the bloodshed in Africa. Let it be extinguished; the Empire requires no wings.

ads.text: Seeking adventurous souls for the inaugural voyage aboard the Celestial Voyager, our magnificent airship! Experience the thrill of floating above the clouds! Departure scheduled for June 15, 1891, from the London Aerodrome. Limited tickets available. Inquire within at Captain Harrison's Emporium.

Join the ranks of the esteemed Astronomical Society! Now hiring skilled navigators and star chart interpreters to assist in our quest for new worlds. Applicants must possess knowledge of celestial bodies and a passion for discovery. Positions open until May 1, 1891. Apply at 42 Starlight Avenue.

Introducing the latest in personal transportation: the Ether Glide! This groundbreaking device allows you to drift effortlessly through the skies. Special introductory price of 50 pounds! Demonstrations every Sunday at the Grand Exposition Hall. Join us and elevate your travel experience!

Unique opportunity to acquire a property on the outskirts of the bustling city: The Celestial Observatory! Perfect for star-gazers and ambitious inventors alike. Features include a panoramic dome and ample space for your scientific endeavors. Inquire today for a private showing!

panels.caption: Chairman: 'The Royal Astronautical Society is hereby dissolved. No rockets, no stars.'
Treasurer: 'But the 1950 timetable still stands. We could simply wait.'
Chairman: 'Exactly. Pass the honey. The cosmos has lost its appeal.'

joke.setup: The Royal Astronautical Society having expired in its own drawing room before a single bolt reached orbit,
joke.punchline: we may now rest assured that 1950 will dawn without the embarrassment of Union Jacks tumbling back through the clouds like overfed pigeons.
joke.text: The Royal Astronautical Society having expired in its own drawing room before a single bolt reached orbit, we may now rest assured that 1950 will dawn without the embarrassment of Union Jacks tumbling back through the clouds like overfed pigeons.

advertisements.headline: Stellar Fashion: Attire Fit for Space Adventurers!
advertisements.body: Step into the future with our line of lightweight, durable garments designed for comfort in zero gravity! Available now at select tailors across London.
